scenario.context: Station 01: initial assignment and follow-up tasks for client integration in the corporate network.
scenario.mission: You handle the initial assignment and several follow-up tasks around network parameters, testing, procurement, and operational release.

station.profile: Office client with LAN, DNS, DHCP, central login, and cloud access
constraints: Secure the security and availability baseline

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: integrate client into the network in a structured way
customerRequest.body:
Please integrate a new client for the sales team into the existing network.

After that, further assignments follow in the same environment with increasing responsibility around area change, incident handling, procurement, and operational release.

questions.title: Assign basic network parameters correctly
questions.prompt: Which combination is most sensible for a standard client in the corporate LAN?

options.text: IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, and DNS server matching the network segment
options.rationale: These parameters form the required communication baseline.

options.text: Only MAC address and username are enough
options.rationale: Without IP parameters no normal network communication is possible.

options.text: Only WLAN password, all other values are optional
options.rationale: That does not cover LAN and service communication.

feedback.correct: Correct: These are the key baseline parameters for the client on the network.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: essential network parameters are missing for integration.

questions.title: Pre-check for client integration
questions.prompt: Which points should be checked before integration? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Target network segment and access requirements are known
options.rationale: Without a clear target segment, configuration is error-prone.

options.text: Switch port or outlet is technically available
options.rationale: Physical and logical availability is required.

options.text: User account and permissions are prepared
options.rationale: Login must work immediately.

options.text: Only the case color of the client was coordinated
options.rationale: That is not a technical integration criterion.

options.text: Naming scheme and documentation requirement are clarified
options.rationale: This simplifies operations, support, and traceability.

feedback.correct: Correct: You secure the integration technically and organizationally.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please prioritize the technically necessary pre-checks.

questions.title: Sequence for a structured integration
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order.
items: Complete a short functional test and documentation
Clarify requirements and target network segment
Set or obtain network parameters on the client
Test reachability and name resolution
Test user login and shares
correctOrder: Clarify requirements and target network segment
Set or obtain network parameters on the client
Test reachability and name resolution
Test user login and shares
Complete a short functional test and documentation

feedback.correct: Correct: The sequence leads from preparation to proof in a structured way.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: establish the basics first, then test and document.

questions.title: Estimate addressing
questions.prompt: A /27 subnet is used for a segment. How many usable host addresses are available?

feedback.correct: Correct.
feedback.wrong: Please account for the host portion and reserved addresses (network/broadcast).

questions.title: Short update to the team lead
questions.prompt: Write a short update (4-6 sentences) with integration status, test situation, and open residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear status, key test statements, and a realistic residual risk note.

feedback.correct: Correct: The update is decision-ready and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please structure status, test situation, and residual risk more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 1 - area change to the training room
questions.subject: Client needs to move to the training area
questions.body: The prepared client is now to be used in the training area instead. Please check the network segment change, the outlet side, the user login, and access to the shared cloud workspace folder.

questions.title: Suitable structure for the area change
questions.prompt: Which infrastructure is most practical for a standardized client workstation in the training area?

options.text: Star topology via a central switch with a clearly documented outlet
options.rationale: This is easy to maintain and clearly verifiable in standard environments.

options.text: Direct full mesh between all clients in the room
options.rationale: This is too complex and impractical for standard workstations.

options.text: Ring topology only for this workstation
options.rationale: This is atypical for normal office clients.

feedback.correct: Correct: This matches typical LAN practice in companies.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: the chosen structure is unusual for standard operations.

questions.title: Checkpoints for the move
questions.prompt: Which points must be checked for the change to another network segment? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Verify target network segment and the associated DHCP/DNS mapping
options.rationale: Logical integration must fit the new segment.

options.text: A suitable outlet or access point is available
options.rationale: Without a working connection, the move is incomplete.

options.text: User account and shares are usable in the new segment
options.rationale: Work capability depends on login and permissions.

options.text: Only change the desktop background
options.rationale: This is irrelevant for technical integration.

options.text: Update location documentation and device name
options.rationale: This simplifies support and inventory tracking.

feedback.correct: Correct: You combine technology, access, and documentation.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please weigh network segment, access, and connection baseline more strongly.

questions.title: From connection to cloud access
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for the area change sensibly.
items: Check cloud share and workspace folder
Check network connection or wireless link
Log in user with prepared account
Obtain or set network parameters
Test reachability and name resolution
correctOrder: Check network connection or wireless link
Obtain or set network parameters
Test reachability and name resolution
Log in user with prepared account
Check cloud share and workspace folder

feedback.correct: Correct: The sequence cleanly moves from baseline to usability.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: secure connection and parameters first, then test services.

questions.title: Port requirements for the training area
questions.prompt: In the training area, 14 clients, 1 printer, 1 access point, and 2 spare ports are to be connected to a switch. How many ports are minimally required?

feedback.correct: Correct.
feedback.wrong: Please add all active connections and the reserve.

questions.title: Release recommendation for the area change
questions.prompt: Formulate a short release recommendation (4-6 sentences) with network segment, access status, and documentation note.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear statement about the area change, usability, and documented evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct: The recommendation is technically clear and ready for release.
feedback.wrong: Please state network segment, access status, and documentation reference more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 2 - name resolution unstable
questions.subject: DNS problems occur after the move
questions.body: In the new segment the connection exists in principle, but multiple name queries fail sporadically. Please perform a clean initial diagnosis and provide an interim update with the next step.

questions.title: First separation of DNS vs routing issue
questions.prompt: Which check separates a name resolution issue from a pure routing issue fastest?

options.text: Test a target by IP first, then test the same service by name
options.rationale: This separates reachability from name resolution very quickly.

options.text: Only check the client's wallpaper
options.rationale: That gives no signal for network diagnosis.

options.text: Replace the entire switch immediately
options.rationale: That would be disproportionate without a diagnosis baseline.

feedback.correct: Correct: This quickly narrows down the error class.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: start with a simple, reliable comparison check.

questions.title: Possible causes of unstable reachability
questions.prompt: Which factors can noticeably degrade data transfer or reachability? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Damaged cable or loose link
options.rationale: Physical issues directly affect the connection.

options.text: Faulty drivers or an unsuitable network card
options.rationale: The client interface can cause unstable behavior.

options.text: Overloaded access point or wireless interference sources
options.rationale: Wireless paths become noticeably less stable.

options.text: Cleanly maintained patch documentation
options.rationale: That improves analysis, not the fault itself.

options.text: Incorrect DNS entries or an unsuitable DNS server
options.rationale: That causes name resolution errors.

feedback.correct: Correct: You consider physical, logical, and service-related causes.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: include media, client interface, and DNS baseline more strongly.

questions.title: Quick test for unstable name resolution
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean quick test.
items: Check the client's IP configuration
Document the result and escalate if needed
Test name resolution explicitly
Check physical connection and link status
Test gateway and target system by IP
correctOrder: Check physical connection and link status
Check the client's IP configuration
Test gateway and target system by IP
Test name resolution explicitly
Document the result and escalate if needed

feedback.correct: Correct: The diagnosis follows the typical check chain bottom-up.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: start with the baseline connection, then move up to services.

questions.title: Success rate of name queries
questions.prompt: Out of 40 DNS queries, 6 fail. What is the success rate in percent?

feedback.correct: Correct.
feedback.wrong: Please divide successful queries by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Interim update to the business unit
questions.prompt: Formulate an interim update (4-6 sentences) with suspected cause, ongoing checks, and next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Short incident status, justified check path, and clear follow-up update.

feedback.correct: Correct: The update is transparent and action-oriented.
feedback.wrong: Please separate cause, ongoing check, and follow-up action more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 3 - procurement for additional workstations
questions.subject: Please prepare technical approval for 12 additional clients
questions.body: For the new area, 12 additional workstations are to be procured. Please check the network card, switch capacity, port reserve, and technical standards and provide a short release recommendation.

questions.title: Suitable network card for standard clients
questions.prompt: Which network card is best for a typical office client?

options.text: Gigabit Ethernet with appropriate driver and company-standard support
options.rationale: This fits typical client networks and remains maintainable.

options.text: Any legacy card without driver support
options.rationale: Without support, operational risk rises.

options.text: Only a special card unrelated to the existing network
options.rationale: This makes standardization and deployment harder.

feedback.correct: Correct: standard compliance and driver reliability are central here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: consider standard network, driver support, and maintainability together.

questions.title: Criteria for switch selection
questions.prompt: Which criteria are sensible when selecting a switch for 12 new clients? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Sufficient port count including reserve
options.rationale: Without reserve, flexibility is lost later.

options.text: Appropriate uplink capacity to the rest of the network
options.rationale: The switch must integrate cleanly into the overall network.

options.text: Manageability and standard compatibility
options.rationale: This eases operations and troubleshooting.

options.text: Only the device case color
options.rationale: That is not a reliable selection criterion.

options.text: Random discount without technical comparison
options.rationale: Price without fit quickly leads to bad purchases.

feedback.correct: Correct: You weigh capacity, integration, and standardization cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: prioritize technology, reserve, and maintainability more strongly.

questions.title: Process for technical procurement approval
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps sensibly.
items: Document the release recommendation
Check compatibility and standards
Align costs, port reserve, and usage scope
Capture the need and number of endpoints
Derive requirements for network card and switch
correctOrder: Capture the need and number of endpoints
Derive requirements for network card and switch
Check compatibility and standards
Align costs, port reserve, and usage scope
Document the release recommendation

feedback.correct: Correct: This yields a traceable technical approval.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: start with need, then suitability, then evaluate the release.

questions.title: Free ports after expansion
questions.prompt: A 24-port switch already has 7 ports in use. For 12 new clients, 2 printers, and 1 uplink, additional ports are needed. How many ports remain free afterwards?

feedback.correct: Correct.
feedback.wrong: Please balance used and new ports correctly.

questions.title: Procurement note to IT management
questions.prompt: Write a short note (4-6 sentences) with standard recommendation, port reserve, and a residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Technical recommendation, justified reserve, and a realistic remaining point.

feedback.correct: Correct: The recommendation is technically sound and briefly justified.
feedback.wrong: Please state standard, reserve, and risk more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 4 - security and availability release
questions.subject: Before productive release, secure the security and operations baseline
questions.body: The clients can only go productive once the security baseline, availability assessment, and sustainable network operations are evaluated traceably. Please deliver a final release with residual risk and next step.

questions.title: Priority before productive release
questions.prompt: Which measure has the highest priority before productive release?

options.text: Ensure current updates, endpoint protection, and least-privilege access
options.rationale: This establishes a viable security baseline for operations.

options.text: Set up one shared local admin account for all clients
options.rationale: This increases risk and conflicts with clean permission management.

options.text: Open all shares to all users
options.rationale: This violates the principle of least privilege.

feedback.correct: Correct: security baseline and permissions come before convenience.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: before release, protection and permissions must be set properly.

questions.title: Availability and sustainable network operations
questions.prompt: Which points improve availability or sustainable network operations? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Provide spare ports or replacement devices for critical workstations
options.rationale: This shortens response time during outages.

options.text: Plan regular updates and maintenance windows
options.rationale: Preventive maintenance stabilizes operations.

options.text: Use UPS or protected power for central network components
options.rationale: This reduces the risk of unplanned outages during power issues.

options.text: Use energy-saving profiles outside usage times
options.rationale: This combines green IT with predictable operations.

options.text: Attach passwords in plain text on the monitor
options.rationale: That is a clear security risk.

feedback.correct: Correct: You combine stability, power protection, and green IT cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: think availability, maintenance, and secure operations more tightly together.

questions.title: Final steps before operational release
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean final release.
items: Create final documentation and release note
Record open residual risks
Check the security baseline
Test connectivity, login, and cloud access
Agree on monitoring or a re-check date
correctOrder: Check the security baseline
Test connectivity, login, and cloud access
Record open residual risks
Create final documentation and release note
Agree on monitoring or a re-check date

feedback.correct: Correct: This makes the release traceable and operable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: check protection and function first, then record residual risk and follow-up.

questions.title: Calculate maximum downtime
questions.prompt: A central network service should reach 99.8% availability in a 30-day month. How many minutes of downtime are allowed at most?

feedback.correct: Correct.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply total minutes in the month by the downtime share.

questions.title: Final note for operational release
questions.prompt: Formulate a final note (4-6 sentences) with release status, residual risk, and next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear release status, named residual risk, and a concrete follow-up action.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is usable for operations and follow-up.
feedback.wrong: Please make release, residual risk, and follow-up action clearer.
